turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having some weird stuff going on with my chest. It's been tight and I get winded easily, even when I'm just doing normal things like walking around the house or driving.

turns.student: Can you tell me more about what you've been feeling?
turns.patient: It started three days ago, I think. At first, it was just a little tightness in my chest, but over the past two days, it's gotten worse and I feel like I'm getting winded really easily, even when I'm not doing anything strenuous. It's pretty scary to be honest.

turns.student: When you have those episodes, do you notice anything else happening to your body?
turns.patient: To be honest, it feels like my heart is racing a bit faster than usual and it's hard for me to catch my breath. Other than that, I just feel really anxious and worried about what might be going on.

turns.student: Any sweating or clamminess when the symptoms hit?
turns.patient: No, there's no sweating or anything like that. It's just a general feeling of anxiety and discomfort in my chest. (pauses) Actually, now that you mention it, I did feel a little sweaty during one of the episodes yesterday, but it was mild and only lasted for a few minutes.

turns.student: So no perspiration at all during the episodes?
turns.patient: No, not really. Just a little bit like I said, during that one episode yesterday. But nothing significant. It's mostly just this tightness in my chest and feeling winded. (pauses) And to be honest, it's been going on for so long now, even when I'm sitting still or lying down, which is pretty unusual for me.

turns.student: Does the discomfort stay in one spot, or does it move around?
turns.patient: The tightness stays right in my chest, usually just below the breastbone. It's a bit hard to describe, but it feels like pressure building up inside there. Sometimes it feels like it's radiating out to my left arm, but that's not always the case. It's pretty localized to my chest area most of the time.

turns.student: Does it radiate anywhere — like your arms, neck, or jaw?
turns.patient: Yeah, I mentioned earlier that sometimes it feels like it's radiating out to my left arm, but only during the more severe episodes. It's not a sharp pain or anything, just a kind of tingling sensation in my arm when the pressure builds up in my chest.

turns.student: Any tingling or numbness in your arms when it happens?
turns.patient: I did mention that earlier, didn't I? Yeah, during the more severe episodes, I get this tingling sensation in my left arm. It's not a big deal, but it's definitely there and can be a bit unsettling.

turns.student: What about your extremities — any tingling, pain, or unusual sensations?
turns.patient: I don't feel anything out of the ordinary in my feet or legs, just some mild swelling in my ankles that's been going on for a while. It's nothing too serious, just feels like my shoes are a bit tighter than usual because of it.

turns.student: Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Ankles, feet, hands?
turns.patient: Yeah, I mentioned earlier that I've had some mild swelling in my ankles, but other than that, I don't notice anything unusual. It's just that my shoes feel a bit tighter than usual because of it. Nothing too concerning, just kind of annoying, you know?

turns.student: Do your shoes feel tighter than usual, or any puffiness in your legs?
turns.patient: Yeah, my shoes definitely feel tighter than usual, and I've noticed that my ankles are a bit puffy, especially when I wake up in the morning. It's not super painful, just kind of uncomfortable.

turns.student: Any stomach issues during the episodes? Nausea, queasiness, upset stomach?
turns.patient: To be honest, yeah, sometimes I get a little bit of nausea during those episodes. It's not like I'm going to throw up or anything, but it's just this weird feeling in my stomach that goes along with the chest tightness and shortness of breath. But it's not always there, maybe half the time or so?

turns.student: Do you feel sick to your stomach when the symptoms come on?
turns.patient: Yeah, I'd say about half the time, I get this mild nausea that goes along with the chest tightness and shortness of breath. It's not like a full-blown stomachache or anything, just a weird, queasy feeling that passes after the episode is over.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
turns.patient: No, I haven't experienced any of those symptoms. It's all been pretty much limited to this chest tightness and shortness of breath, with some mild nausea like we just discussed. Other than that, I feel fine, no fevers, chills, or night sweats at all.

turns.student: Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or weight?
turns.patient: To be honest, my appetite has been a bit off lately. I've been feeling really anxious about what's going on with my chest, so I've been eating less than usual. And as for weight, I haven't really noticed anything too significant, but I have been getting up to use the bathroom more often at night, which might be related to the swelling in my ankles or something else entirely.

turns.student: Tell me about your lifestyle — diet, exercise, daily routine.
turns.patient: To be honest, my lifestyle isn't exactly great. I'm a long-haul truck driver, so I spend most of my week on the road and don't really have time to exercise or eat healthy meals. My diet is pretty heavy in fast food - I'd say 5-6 days a week I'm eating at restaurants or grabbing something quick on the go. I know it's not great for me, but it's just what my schedule allows. And as for exercise, well... let's just say I don't get much of that either.

turns.student: How active are you physically? Do you work out or walk regularly?
turns.patient: I'm pretty sedentary, to be honest. As a truck driver, my job is mostly sitting and driving for long periods of time. I might get some light walking in when I stop at rest stops or restaurants, but that's about it. I don't really have time for regular exercise, and to be frank, I just haven't made it a priority.

turns.student: What does a typical day of eating look like for you?
turns.patient: My diet is pretty predictable, to be honest. Like I said, most days are fast food or restaurant meals. It's usually burgers, fries, pizza - that kind of stuff. Sometimes I'll grab a salad or something if I'm feeling guilty about my eating habits, but it's not a regular thing. My wife tries to cook healthy meals for me when I'm home on weekends, but during the week, it's just whatever's quick and easy.

turns.student: Any medical problems in your family? Parents, siblings?
turns.patient: My dad had a heart attack when he was 52 years old, unfortunately. He passed away from it. My mom has hypertension and type 2 diabetes, but she's still alive and doing okay at 78. That's about it in terms of family medical history that I'm aware of.

turns.student: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious diseases?
turns.patient: Yeah, my dad had a heart attack when he was 52 years old. He didn't survive it. It's been on my mind a lot lately, to be honest, given what I'm going through with my chest tightness and shortness of breath.
